# Standard Formula Samples (Revised: No Overlap in Sovereign-Minister-Assistant-Envoy)

## Guizhi Decoction (Cinnamon Twig Decoction)

### Ingredients
Guizhi (Cinnamon Twig) 3 liang (peeled), Shaoyao (Peony Root) 3 liang, Gancao (Licorice) 2 liang (roasted),
Shengjiang (Fresh Ginger) 3 liang (sliced), Dazao (Jujube) 12 pieces (pitted).

### Decoction and Administration
Take the five ingredients, coarsely crush three of them.
Add 7 sheng of water, simmer gently over low heat until 3 sheng remains.
Strain out the dregs. Adjust to warm temperature and take 1 sheng.

Shortly after taking, drink about 1 sheng of warm thin congee to assist the medicinal effect.
Cover warmly for about one hour, until slight sweating appears all over the body—this is optimal.
Do not allow profuse sweating, otherwise the illness will not be cured.

If sweating occurs and the illness resolves after one dose, stop the remaining doses;
there is no need to finish the entire preparation.
If no sweating occurs, take another dose following the same method.
If still no sweating, shorten the interval between doses;
finish three doses within about half a day.

For severe cases, administer day and night, observing around the clock.
If symptoms remain after one full preparation, prepare another.
If sweating still does not occur, administer up to two or three preparations.

### Functions and Indications
Relieves the exterior and promotes muscle relaxation, regulates Ying and Wei.
Indicated for Taiyang wind-strike: Yang floating and Yin weak.
Yang floating leads to spontaneous fever; Yin weakness leads to spontaneous sweating.
Chills, aversion to wind, mild fever, nasal congestion, and nausea.

### Sovereign-Minister-Assistant-Envoy Analysis
- Sovereign: Guizhi (Cinnamon Twig), pungent-warm, relieves the exterior, warms and unblocks the channels.
- Minister: Shaoyao (Peony Root), sour-bitter slightly cold, nourishes Yin and astringes Ying.
- Assistant: Shengjiang (Fresh Ginger), pungent-warm, assists Guizhi in releasing the exterior;
  Dazao (Jujube), sweet-warm, tonifies Qi and fortifies the spleen.
- Envoy: Gancao (Licorice), sweet-warm, harmonizes all medicinals.

---

## Mahuang Decoction (Ephedra Decoction)

### Ingredients
Mahuang (Ephedra) 3 liang (nodes removed), Guizhi (Cinnamon Twig) 2 liang (peeled),
Gancao (Licorice) 1 liang (roasted), Xingren (Apricot Kernel) 70 pieces (skin and tip removed).

### Decoction and Administration
Take the four ingredients. Add 9 sheng of water.
First decoct Mahuang until 2 sheng evaporates; remove the froth.
Add the other medicinals and decoct until 2.5 sheng remains.
Strain out the dregs. Take 8 he warm, cover to induce slight sweating.
No need for warm congee. For the rest, follow the same instructions as Guizhi Decoction.

### Functions and Indications
Induces sweating to release the exterior, diffuses the lung and relieves asthma.
Indicated for Taiyang disease: headache, fever, body pain, lumbar pain, joint pain,
aversion to wind, absence of sweating, and wheezing.

### Sovereign-Minister-Assistant-Envoy Analysis
- Sovereign: Mahuang (Ephedra), pungent-warm, induces sweating to release the exterior, diffuses the lung and relieves asthma.
- Minister: Guizhi (Cinnamon Twig), pungent-warm, relieves the exterior, warms and unblocks the channels.
- Assistant: Xingren (Apricot Kernel), bitter-warm, descends lung Qi, relieves asthma and cough.
- Envoy: Gancao (Licorice), sweet-warm, harmonizes all medicinals.

---

## Xiaochaihu Decoction (Minor Bupleurum Decoction)

### Ingredients
Chaihu (Bupleurum) 8 liang, Huangqin (Scutellaria) 3 liang,
Renshen (Ginseng) 3 liang, Gancao (Licorice) 3 liang (roasted),
Shengjiang (Fresh Ginger) 3 liang (sliced), Dazao (Jujube) 12 pieces (pitted),
Banxia (Pinellia) 0.5 sheng (washed).

### Decoction and Administration
Take the seven ingredients. Add 12 sheng of water, decoct until 6 sheng remains.
Strain out the dregs, then decoct again until 3 sheng remains.
Take 1 sheng warm, three times a day.

### Functions and Indications
Harmonizes Shaoyang.
Indicated for cold damage (Shanghan) for five or six days with wind-strike:
alternating chills and fever, fullness in the chest and hypochondria,
depressed appetite, vexation and frequent vomiting.
Other possible presentations:
chest vexation without vomiting, thirst, abdominal pain,
hardness below the hypochondria, palpitations, dysuria,
absence of thirst with mild fever, or cough.

### Sovereign-Minister-Assistant-Envoy Analysis
- Sovereign: Chaihu (Bupleurum), bitter-neutral, harmonizes Shaoyang, courses the liver and gallbladder.
- Minister: Huangqin (Scutellaria), bitter-cold, clears and drains Shaoyang heat.
- Assistant: Renshen (Ginseng) and Dazao (Jujube), tonify Qi and fortify the spleen;
  Banxia (Pinellia) and Shengjiang (Fresh Ginger), harmonize the stomach and direct rebellious Qi downward.
- Envoy: Gancao (Licorice), harmonizes all medicinals.
